steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says inefficient redox reactions bottleneck electron transfer in MFCs, limiting power. Adding silver more than doubles output. The researchers' hypothesis must explain how silver's conductivity overcomes that bottleneck.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "silver nanoparticles may increase the metabolic processes of the bacteria, thereby increasing the number of free electrons available to transfer to the electrode.": The bottleneck is transfer efficiency, not electron quantity. More electrons don't help if transfer is still inefficient. ✗
  • "electrons may be conducted directly to the electrode before the silver nanoparticles catalyze the redox reactions.": Reverses the logical sequence; conductivity is the property silver is known for, not a downstream effect. ✗
  • "as the density of the biofilm increases, the series of redox reactions may accelerate independent of the presence of the silver nanoparticles.": Density is not the stated bottleneck; inefficient redox reactions are. ✗
  • "silver nanoparticles may allow electrons to bypass the series of redox reactions and transfer directly to the electrode.": Directly addresses the stated bottleneck by allowing electrons to transfer to the electrode without relying on slow redox steps. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
